steps.title: Identify the key constraint in the sediment evidence
steps.content: The strongest signal is that fine mudstone—the predominant sediment type—shows episodic desiccation cracks. This reveals two requirements: a persistent water environment (to deposit mudstone) that periodically dried, plus the coarse sandstone intervals indicate flowing water at times.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly proposes this: a prolonged lake that occasionally dried with intermittent streams. It's the only choice that explains all three features of the evidence in a coherent sequence. B reverses the temporal order, C contradicts the desiccation data, and D eliminates the lake.

That matches choice A—A is correct.
